prompt: A traditional Japanese zen rock garden (karesansui). Carefully raked white gravel forms concentric circle patterns, with asymmetric groups of moss-covered stones placed outside the circles. A bamboo water feature (shishi-odoshi) sits to the right. A bamboo fence and the edge of a wooden veranda form the boundary. Soft overcast light with muted monochrome grey tones. To the left, a stone basin (tsukubai) with a bamboo dipper sits beneath a low-hanging maple branch with green leaves. Further left along the veranda, sliding shoji screens open into a tatami room with a low table. To the right beyond the shishi-odoshi, a stone path of stepping stones leads around the corner of the building toward a hidden garden gate. First-person viewer. First-person view from the veranda edge looking out across the raked gravel patterns toward the stones and bamboo fence. No held objects.

scene_adherence.visible_part: This is a traditional Japanese zen rock garden (karesansui). Carefully raked white gravel forms concentric circle patterns, with asymmetric groups of moss-covered stones placed outside the circles. A bamboo water feature (shishi-odoshi) sits to the right. A bamboo fence and the edge of a wooden veranda form the boundary. The scene has soft overcast light with muted monochrome grey tones.

Further left along the veranda, sliding shoji screens open into a tatami room with a low table. To the right beyond the shishi-odoshi, a stone path of stepping stones leads around the corner of the building toward a hidden garden gate.
